zh.lifeSummary: 當Mathilda 'Maud' D'Arques於1057年出生在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利阿爾可-拉-巴塔爾時，她的父親Guillaume d'Arques22歲，母親Beatrice Malet12歲。她於1087年與Guillaume I de Tancarville - Chamberlain d'Normandie et d'Angleterre在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利阿爾可-拉-巴塔爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1112年享年55歲，埋葬在法國諾門迪艾法圖維爾-格雷斯當。

nameMeanings.name: D'Arques
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Carlos, Mario, Luis, Fernando, Jorge, Armando, Ana, Joaquin, Julio, Marcos.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Paulo, Henrique, Lourenco, Seraphine, Vasco.

Catalan (Marquès); Spanish and southern French (Marqués): from Catalan marquès, Spanish and Occitan marqués ‘marquis’, words of French origin (see Marquis ), applied either as a nickname for someone who behaved like a marquis or as an occupational name for a servant in the household of a marquis.
Portuguese: patronymic from the personal name   Marcos  .

variants.name: Marquis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Normand, Marcel, Fernand, Lucien, Rosaire, Andre, Armand, Camille, Cecile, Jacques, Francois.

French: from the title marquis ‘marquess’, applied as an ironic nickname to someone who behaved like a marquis or as a metonymic occupational name for a servant in the household of a marquis. The title, derived from an ancient Germanic word meaning ‘boundary’ (compare March ), originally referred to the governor of a border territory. Marquises did not form part of the original French feudal structure of nobility; the title was first adopted by the Counts of Toulouse because of their possessions in the border region beyond the Rhône. Compare Markee and Markey .

English: nickname from Middle English markis ‘marquis, marquess, nobleman’ (Old French marchis); see also 1 above. The word is also found as a female personal name which may also be origin of the surname.

nameMeanings.name: Mathilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: 'Maud'
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Mathilda 'Maud' D'Arques was born in 1057, in Arques-la-Bataille, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France, her father, Guillaume d'Arques, was 22 and her mother, Beatrice Malet, was 12. She married Guillaume I de Tancarville - Chamberlain d'Normandie et d'Angleterre in 1087, in Arques-la-Bataille, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 6 daughters. She died in 1112, at the age of 55, and was buried in Fatouville-Grestain, Eure, Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mathilda 'Maud' D'Arques est née en 1057, à Arques-la-Bataille, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France, son père, Guillaume d'Arques, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Beatrice Malet, avait 12 ans. Elle a épousé Guillaume I de Tancarville - Chamberlain d'Normandie et d'Angleterre en 1087, à Arques-la-Bataille, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée en 1112, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterrée à Fatouville-Grestain, Eure, Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mathilda 'Maud' D'Arques 1057 in Arques-la-Bataille, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Guillaume d'Arques 22 und ihre Mutter Beatrice Malet 12. Sie hat 1087 in Arques-la-Bataille, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich Guillaume I de Tancarville - Chamberlain d'Normandie et d'Angleterre geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb 1112 im Alter von 55 Jahren und wurde in Fatouville-Grestain, Eure, Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

ko.Thorpe Arche, Yorkshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 소 인 로스웰
ko.lifeSummary: Mathilda 'Maud' D'Arques(이)가 1057년 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 아크스-라-바테이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guillaume d'Arques(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrice Malet(은)는 12세였다. 그녀는 1087년 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 아크스-라-바테이에서 Guillaume I de Tancarville - Chamberlain d'Normandie et d'Angleterre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1112년 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 노먼디 우르 팻우비레-그레스테인에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Guillaume I de Tancarville - Chamberlain d'Normandie et d'Angleterre em 1087, em Arques-la-Bataille, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 6 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mathilda 'Maud' D'Arques родилась в 1057 г. в местности аркуес-ла-батеилл, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция, её отцу по имени Guillaume d'Arques было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Beatrice Malet, 12. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guillaume I de Tancarville - Chamberlain d'Normandie et d'Angleterre в 1087 г. в местности аркуес-ла-батеилл, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла в 1112 г. в возрасте 55 лет и была похоронена в местности фатоувилле-грестеин, эур, норманди, Франция.
